title: Reading the horse's energy — calibration notes
summary: Calibration as conditional inference under partial observability. Multiple low-bandwidth signals (ear position, jaw, breath, footfall) integrated over time produce a high-precision posterior on the horse's state. Improves with rolling baseline.

title: Tail dependence in commodity carry
summary: Calibration as the difference between unconditional and conditional probability. Pearson correlation says 0.1; conditional on equity drawdown, correlation jumps to 0.7. Mean-variance optimisers can't see the conditional structure and over-allocate to tail-dependent strategies.

title: On the texture of attention
summary: Attention isn't quantity, it's texture. Wide-diffuse vs narrow-point vs rhythmic-alternating vs back-of-mind. Each is a different physiological state, not interchangeable. Texture-tuning is a meta-skill; somatic before cognitive.

title: Lateral work session — regime shift at minute 25
summary: Caspian shifts from compliant-mechanical to engaged-offering at minute 25 of a session, given correct rhythm and consistent cues. Sharp transition, not gradual. Cumulative consistency tipping into a phase change in the underlying dynamics.

title: Regime change detection — three competing approaches
summary: Markov-switching too lagged; Bayesian online changepoint too noisy on small samples; embedding-based brittle to novelty. Each method has a different failure mode; ensemble beats any single one. Heterogeneous weak signals over single strong one.

title: Activation steering experiments — daily log
summary: Linear steering toward 'Tuesday' breaks coherence — the day-of-week ring is a real geometric structure, and pushing along the linear average direction exits the manifold into the void. Cross-architecture stability of the failure suggests it's a property of the underlying geometry.

title: The shape of a long bet
summary: Long bets vs short cycles have different shapes. Pre-register what would convince you you're wrong. Worry about motivations more than reasoning. Optionality over conviction. Live feedback on long bets is mostly noise; quality lives in structural fit established at the start.

title: Quant track vs ML research — fork analysis
summary: 10-year career bet under heavy short-cycle noise. Decision quality has to come from structural reasoning, not how this week went. Hybrid path preserves optionality: ship the geodesic-retrieval bench publicly, see who notices, re-evaluate at year mark.

title: Bali workation — venue research
summary: Three weeks in Bali to write the long-form piece. Constraints: internet for video calls with Brisbane, real desk ergonomics, climate. Real question: is workation the right shape? Robin's point: optimising for cool environment, under-optimising for writing constraints.

title: Why we picked Postgres over Mongo — postmortem
summary: Hierarchical data with relational constraints is still relational data. Mongo's pitch is for hierarchical data without relational constraints — most data isn't actually that. Match storage to constraints, not surface structure.

title: Trailer loading reset training plan
summary: Caspian's bad trailer experience created a Bayesian update toward trailer aversion. Reset is also a series of Bayesian updates — feed the prior new evidence in a way it can integrate. Forcing through the trailer would generate more aversive evidence and reinforce the wrong association.

title: Dog walker rota — scheduling logic
summary: Scheduling problems are constraint-satisfaction problems pretending to be people problems. Cancellation policy with skin-in-the-game forces 48h calendar review. Same shape as research priorities — values question wearing constraint-satisfaction costume.
